title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword
trailerQuery: Onimusha Way of the Sword official trailer
description:
Capcom revives its legendary samurai-horror series for the first time in two decades. Set in a demon-corrupted Edo-period Kyoto, Way of the Sword follows a swordsman whose likeness is modeled on the iconic actor Toshiro Mifune, wielding the supernatural Oni Gauntlet to absorb the souls of slain enemies.

The game fuses precise, weighty katana combat with the dark, atmospheric dread the original Onimusha was known for, pitting the player against the demonic Genma. Built on Capcom's RE Engine, it promises the same cinematic production values that made the recent Resident Evil remakes so striking - a natural watch for anyone who follows brooding, combat-driven action on the channel.

title: Pragmata
description:
One of Capcom's most mysterious projects, Pragmata is a brand-new sci-fi IP set on a near-future research station on the Moon. Players control Hugh, a veteran astronaut, accompanied by Diana, a curious android child who appears entirely human.

The game's signature hook is its dual-layered combat: players fight in real time while simultaneously solving hacking puzzles on a heads-up interface, blending action and problem-solving in a way few games attempt. After several years of delays, Capcom's atmospheric, story-driven adventure is finally on track - a slower, more cerebral counterpoint to the channel's action-heavy lineup.

title: The Blood of Dawnwalker
trailerQuery: The Blood of Dawnwalker official trailer
description:
The debut title from Rebel Wolves - a studio founded by senior developers behind The Witcher 3 - The Blood of Dawnwalker is a narrative sandbox RPG set in a plague-ravaged, vampire-haunted 14th-century Europe. Players take the role of Coen, a young man caught between worlds as a Dawnwalker, able to walk in daylight as a human and hunt by night with vampiric power.

Its standout idea is a story bound by a ticking clock: a finite number of days and nights in which to act, forcing genuine consequence into every choice. For viewers who enjoyed the channel's darker, story-led RPGs like Hogwarts Legacy and Banishers, this is one to watch closely.

title: Marvel's Wolverine
description:
From Insomniac Games - the studio behind the channel's Marvel's Spider-Man walkthroughs - comes a darker, more brutal Marvel adventure. Marvel's Wolverine is a single-player, PS5-exclusive action game starring Logan, casting players into savage, visceral combat that leans fully into the character's mature, ferocious nature.

Expect the same cinematic storytelling and polished set-piece direction that made Insomniac's Spider-Man titles such strong walkthrough material, now in service of a grittier, mature tale about a man hunting for the truth of his own past. It is one of the most anticipated PlayStation exclusives on the horizon.

title: Grand Theft Auto VI
trailerQuery: Grand Theft Auto VI official trailer
description:
The most anticipated game in the world. Grand Theft Auto VI returns Rockstar to the neon-soaked state of Leonida and its centerpiece, a modern reimagining of Vice City. For the first time in the series, the story follows a dual-protagonist pair - Lucia and Jason - in a Bonnie-and-Clyde tale of crime, loyalty and ambition, with Lucia the franchise's first female lead.

Rockstar's trademark blend of sprawling open-world freedom, sharp satire and blockbuster cinematic storytelling makes its story mode a natural fit for a full-game walkthrough. After a high-profile delay, it is now locked for a November 19, 2026 launch on PS5.

title: Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet
trailerQuery: Intergalactic The Heretic Prophet official trailer Naughty Dog
description:
Naughty Dog - the studio behind The Last of Us and Uncharted, both pillars of this channel - returns with its first wholly new IP in over a decade. Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet is a single-player sci-fi adventure directed by Neil Druckmann, following bounty hunter Jordan A. Mun, stranded on the long-isolated planet Sempiria.

Early footage points to Naughty Dog's hallmark strengths: character-driven storytelling, striking art direction and grounded, cinematic action. Still some way off, it is shaping up to be one of PlayStation's defining releases of 2027 - and a must-watch the moment it lands.
